page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n1.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15839.14 and a PassMark CPU score of 16949.88. However, single-core processing is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1106.89. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth reaches an average of 1253.98 GB/sec, the overall PassMark memory score falls into the bottom 25% tier. Large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are measured at 106.51 GB/sec and 48.1 GB/sec, respectively.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is structured for compute-focused workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It demonstrates average capabilities in database operations, scoring 4290.01 on PassMark database benchmarks, and handles static web serving at a throughput of 10.26 GB/sec. Redis SET operations perform at 21.48 million operations per second. The instance is suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and basic database tasks, offering a dedicated CPU allocation that ensures consistent compute resources without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n1.3xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.0 GB of memory per core, and contains no local storage or GPU resources. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers average multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 15839.14 and a PassMark CPU score of 16949.88, though its single-core performance is weak. Memory bandwidth is moderate, with uncached read speeds of 106.51 GB/sec. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations where dedicated CPU resources are required but high memory-to-CPU ratios or local storage are unnecessary.
